summary: tag_concepts.py: respect human verdicts on same-model re-runs (--respect-reviewed + --supersede-pending)
description:
Re-running the teacher model (Qwen3.5-27B-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf) against the expanded 78-concept taxonomy must not re-emit competing scores for cells humans already adjudicated (~1,860 accepted + ~2,281 rejected). The filter must live at the persistence layer, not the prompt layer, so the teacher's judgment stays unbiased.

Context: branch `taxonomy-update` currently has 16 new concepts staged in concepts/taxonomy.toml (62 → 78), uncommitted. This ticket must ship and merge before the Qwen v2-taxonomy re-run is launched.

THE PLAN

1. Add `--respect-reviewed` flag to scripts/tag_concepts.py, defaulting ON. Before inserting a staged_tag row, skip if any prior row for (chunk_id, concept_id, model) has `status != 'pending'`. Filter on the negation, not an allowlist — covers accepted, rejected, reassigned, and any future non-pending statuses. Scope is same-model only; different models always emit (a new student model deserves a fresh look).

2. Add `--supersede-pending` flag, defaulting ON. When v2 emits a row for (chunk_id, concept_id, model) whose only prior row is `status='pending'`, delete the v1 pending row in the same transaction. Latest-pending wins; reviewer never sees two competing pending rows for the same cell.

3. Teacher still runs against the FULL new taxonomy in its prompt — filter happens after JSON parse, before insert. Do not contaminate the teacher's judgment by anchoring it to prior human decisions at the prompt level.

4. Optional later: `--keep-superseded` escape hatch that demotes v1 pending to a new `status='superseded'` instead of deleting — only add if the audit story ever demands it.

NOT DOING (considered and dropped)
- taxonomy_version column on staged_tags. Neither flag needs it; the v1-vs-v2-run partition can be derived from concept_id (new-only set) or, if better provenance is wanted, by adding a `created_at` column (a separate, broader concern). Only justification would be editing an existing concept's definition, which we are not doing.

TESTS NEEDED
- respect-reviewed skips an accepted prior, a rejected prior, and a reassigned prior (all same model).
- respect-reviewed does NOT skip a different-model prior.
- supersede-pending deletes v1 pending and inserts v2 pending atomically.

FILES INVOLVED
- scripts/tag_concepts.py — flag plumbing + filter query + supersede logic
- concepts/taxonomy.toml — already updated on this branch
- tests/test_tag_provenance.py (or new tests/test_respect_reviewed.py) — coverage

DEPENDENCY
Must complete before re-running Qwen 27B against the v2 taxonomy.
